title: Tanker report - Week 29
summary:
VLCC
Rates for 270,000mt Middle East Gulf to China slipped to WS 43.5, down two points. However, with a $50/mt fall in bunker prices over the week, earnings have remained flat. The assessment for 280,000mt Middle East Gulf to US Gulf basis Cape to Cape remains at WS 19 level, while 260,000mt West Africa to China and 270,000mt US Gulf to China both maintained last week's levels at WS 46 and $5m respectively.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: Rates for 270,000mt Middle East Gulf to China slipped to WS 43.5, down two points. However, with a $50/mt fall in bunker prices over the week, earnings have remained flat.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Rates for 130,000mt West Africa to the UK-Continent saw a slight improvement to WS 60 level, while 135,000mt Black Sea to the Mediterranean slipped a couple of points to low WS 70s. The market for 140,000mt Basrah to the Mediterranean has hovered around WS 35 level.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: The Mediterranean sector proved to be weaker this week, with rates for 80,000mt Ceyhan to the Mediterranean now at WS 85, down from WS 92.5 a week ago. Meanwhile, rates remained steady in North-West Europe, with 80,000mt Cross-North Sea at WS 87.5/90 and 100,000mt Baltic to UK-Continent at WS 62.5/65 region.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: The market for 75,000mt Middle East to Japan remained flat at WS 80. 55,000mt Arabian Gulf to Japan ended the week at WS 95 level, a slip of about 2.5 points.
